renamed_from: index.json (2026-07-09): self-describing name. There is exactly one bare index.* in the repo (the root dashboard); every other index carries a suffix saying what it indexes.
scope_note: Indexes 2026 rounds ONLY. The year lives in the path (f1-results/2026/), so this file never goes cumulative. A future cross-season pointer would be a separate f1-results/index_seasons.json at the store root, added only when a second season exists.
canon_rule: CORRECTED 2026-07-23 (Michael): this store holds REAL, official 2026 F1 data. There is NO alternate/invented season canon. Any prior note claiming rounds diverge from real-world 2026 or must be left absent to protect a private storyline was WRONG (a placeholder rationalization) and has been retired. Numbers come from official sources (FIA timing sheets, formula1.com) cross-checked against a second source; the ClickUp race task remains canonical for NARRATIVE only. When in doubt, the real official result wins.
schema: Per-round season store. Each round is its own file in this folder (canonical record for that round). This index lists round order + metadata + which rounds carry a sprint, and carries the cuTaskId join key back to the ClickUp race task (narrative companion). Engine fetches this index, then loads round files. Standings + podium/pole/FL are COMPUTED live, never stored twice.
row_schema: Each entry in a round's `classification` (and `sprint.classification`) is ONE driver's whole weekend, keyed by driverId. Fields: pos (finished/classified, null on DNF/DNS), driverId, driver, team, status (FIN/DNF/DNS/DSQ), points; grid (where they STARTED post-penalty; string 'PL' for a pit-lane start); onRoadPos (where they crossed the line before a post-race penalty, when it differs); qualifying { pos (earned grid slot from the session), q1, q2, q3 (lap-time strings, null when not set or eliminated earlier) }; fastLap (THIS driver's best race lap time, optional, null until dug). Sprint rows mirror the same shape; sprint qualifying lives under the sprint block as sprintQualifying { pos, sq1, sq2, sq3 }. The four position landmarks (qualifying.pos -> grid -> onRoadPos -> pos) are stored separately on purpose; positionsGained is derivable (grid-pos) and never stored. ALL new fields are optional and degrade to dashes, so a round entered without quali still renders.
round_level: Round-level `pole` and `fastestLap` remain the OFFICIAL single-seat credits (pole { driverId, driver, team, time }; fastestLap { driverId, driver, team, time, lap } or null). These are distinct from the per-row qualifying/fastLap fields, which are per-driver. Both may be present; the round-level ones are the headline awards.
fastest_lap_note: Round-level fastestLap corrected against official 2026 sources. Albert Park (r1) fastestLap FIXED 2026-07-23 to Verstappen 1:22.091 (lap 43) — it had wrongly read Antonelli 1:22.417 (the 2026-07-09 CU-sourced 'correction' was itself wrong). Suzuka round-level FL is null (task records it as not confirmed). Silverstone round-level FL still absent pending a dig (real 2026 race FL was Hadjar 1:32.268). Per-driver row `fastLap` is a going-forward dig from a lap-time source; Albert Park's Verstappen row now carries his 1:22.091 as the one known per-driver value.
backfill_status: Enriched per-driver schema (qualifying + grid) reference-implemented on Albert Park (r1). Rounds 2-7 quali + grid backfilled 2026-07-09. Austria (r8) + Silverstone (r9) quali + grid BACKFILLED 2026-07-23 from real official 2026 sources (FIA + formula1.com, cross-checked vs racefans grids) — full Q1/Q2/Q3 by driverId, grids post-penalty (Austria: no penalties, grid = quali order; Silverstone: Gasly qualified 12th, 3-place impeding penalty to grid 15). This SUPERSEDES the prior note that left r8/r9 absent 'because real-world 2026 diverges from this season's canon' — that rule was wrong and is retired (see canon_rule). Per-driver row fastLap still null pending a lap-time source (except Albert Park Verstappen).
provenance_verified: Finishing positions + points re-verified vs the ClickUp race tasks (2026-07-09). Store is canonical for RESULTS; the ClickUp task is canonical for NARRATIVE. OPEN RESULTS-RECONCILIATION ITEMS (flagged, NOT resolved in the 2026-07-23 quali pass): (1) Suzuka store classification is 20 rows (Bearman + Stroll absent) and Miami is 21 rows (Gasly absent) — short of the 22-car grid. (2) Several rounds' store finishing order should be re-checked against real official 2026 results now that the real-data mandate is explicit — e.g. Austria's real-world race result should be confirmed against the store's Russell/Verstappen/Antonelli podium. The 2026-07-23 pass added quali + grid by driverId and corrected Albert Park FL + Austria's filler pole time (was 1:04.312, corrected to 1:06.113); it did NOT rewrite finishing positions. A dedicated results reconciliation pass is the next data job.
